metadata.title:
Mayor Boyd Has Remarkable Views
on Morals and Official Conduct
He Says He Is Going to Take His
Own Time About It.

pages.raw_text:

the complaint in writing of any pri-
vate citizen"


And, Furthermore:


"rhat upon the filing of such rec
ommendation or complaint, at the
next meeting of the city council, reg
ular or special. the city council shall
fix a time for investigating the
charges made, which shall not be less
than three nor more than 10 days
thereafter. And upon proof satisfac-
tory to the council that any law of
the state or an ordinance of the city
has been violated, the council may re-
woke such license by a majority vote
of all the members present"


When asked three days ago what he
was going to do about the fact that
employment agents had been caught
trying to entice girls into a house of
ill repute, and in conspiring to get in
nocent girls between the ages of ii
and 16 years to lead a life of shame,
he said


"Ill have to see the corporation
counsel"


The Press said nothing at the time.
Yesterday he again said he would
have to see the corporation counsel.
He had not done it yet, because both
he and the corporation counsel had
been so busy.


Busy with what?


Is there any business under the
heaven of more Importance than the
protection of the grlhood and the
homes of Spokane?


When pressed and pinned down on
the question as to just what he was
going to do he said:
Il amt going to take any action
right now. I'll tell you that. I'm go
ing to take my own time about It,"


This after not only The Press but
the trades council, a representative
body of men, had officially called the
citys attention to the fact that en-
ployment agents In Spokane were en-
gaging In the most infamous crime
| that is on the calendar.
Then in lodging the reporters
questions this mayor said he would
have to talk to the councilmen; he
would have to see how they felt
about 1t,


That Is the proper course, no doubt.
IF foul murder was committed in
the city, the proper way for the Of
iicer sworn to enforce the law would
be to sit calmly In his Office and wait
for someone to come in and make
written complaint against the muir
derer; then this officer should take his
own time about doing anything; and,
finally, when he did decide to move a
little bit go out and see what this
and that official said about 1t before
he decided whether or not he would
even investigate to see If the charge
against the murderer were true.
| At least it seems that is the Boyd
| idea of official Conducr.
